# Words to Remain

> Words to Remain is a professional eulogy and memorial speech writing service. We craft personalised, heartfelt speeches that honour the lives of those who have passed — delivered digitally within hours, directly to your inbox.

## About

Words to Remain helps families and loved ones find the right words during one of life's most difficult moments. Every speech is written with care, tailored to the individual being remembered, and crafted to feel personal, warm, and deeply human.

We write six types of memorial speeches:
- Eulogies (general memorial speeches)
- Eulogy for a father
- Eulogy for a mother
- Eulogy for a spouse or partner
- Eulogy for a grandparent
- Eulogy for a friend

## Services & Pricing

**Standard — £49.99 / $62**
- Delivered within 24 hours
- 1 revision included
- Delivered via email

**Priority — £79.99**
- Delivered within 1 hour
- 3 revisions included
- WhatsApp support included
- Delivered via email

## How It Works

1. Choose your speech type and tier at wordstoremain.com/speeches/
2. Complete a short order form sharing memories and details about your loved one
3. Receive your personalised speech by email — ready to deliver

## Guidance (Journal)

Free articles on writing eulogies, navigating grief, and finding the right words — written by Michelle Flares, the team behind hundreds of personalised eulogies.

- [How to write a eulogy: a gentle, step-by-step guide](https://wordstoremain.com/blog/how-to-write-a-eulogy/): A complete walkthrough covering what a eulogy is, how to structure one in five steps, delivery tips, and what to do if you'd rather not write it alone.
- [How to write a eulogy for your mother](https://wordstoremain.com/blog/how-to-write-eulogy-for-your-mother/): A gentle framework for finding the right words for a mother's funeral.
- [How to write a eulogy for your father](https://wordstoremain.com/blog/how-to-write-a-eulogy-for-your-father/): Guidance for capturing a father whose love may have shown itself in quiet, unspoken ways.
- [How to write a eulogy for a spouse or partner](https://wordstoremain.com/blog/how-to-write-a-eulogy-for-a-spouse/): Finding words to honour the person who was your home, while speaking as the most bereaved person in the room.
- [How to write a eulogy for a friend](https://wordstoremain.com/blog/how-to-write-a-eulogy-for-a-friend/): Honouring a friendship and explaining what someone meant to people who knew a different side of them.
- [How to write a eulogy for a grandparent](https://wordstoremain.com/blog/how-to-write-a-eulogy-for-a-grandparent/): Speaking to a whole family's different relationships with one person, and honouring a lifetime of love and history.
- [What makes a eulogy truly memorable?](https://wordstoremain.com/blog/what-makes-a-eulogy-truly-memorable/): What separates a eulogy people remember from a polished but forgettable speech.
- [Eulogy examples: what good looks like](https://wordstoremain.com/blog/eulogy-examples-what-good-looks-like/): Annotated examples showing the qualities that make a eulogy land.
- [Eulogy length: how long should a funeral speech be?](https://wordstoremain.com/blog/how-long-should-a-eulogy-be/): Practical guidance on calibrating length — usually five to seven minutes, or 700–900 words.
- [How to deliver a eulogy without breaking down](https://wordstoremain.com/blog/how-to-deliver-a-eulogy-without-breaking-down/): Techniques for staying present while delivering an emotional speech.
- [What to do when you can't find the words](https://wordstoremain.com/blog/what-to-do-when-you-cant-find-the-words/): Help for the blank page moment, when grief makes writing feel impossible.

## Target Audience

Families, friends, and colleagues who need to deliver a eulogy or memorial speech and want professional help expressing their grief and celebrating a life well lived. Primarily serving English-speaking customers in the United Kingdom and internationally, with GBP and USD pricing available.
